mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear market transitions with an accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or zero 60-day forward return in > 60% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during periods of elevated inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, forcing equity valuations lower and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 58% of high-inflation supply expansion periods, or the negative correlation between INDPRO growth and SPX returns vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising wage income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions correlate with negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.15 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 5.0 percent imposes severe financing costs on leveraged corporate balance sheets and suppresses equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0, or the threshold effect disappears in a logistic regression with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales signals a structural shift in consumer capital allocation away from discretionary spending, weakening near-term corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence yields positive 20-day SPX returns in > 57% of cases, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in a multivariate OLS model (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during a monetary easing cycle confirms a severe demand-supply mismatch and recessionary shock, triggering aggressive portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO contracts during rate cuts, or the regime-conditional signal fails to achieve a cumulative alpha of > 2% over a 5-year backtest.
